column1: # Constructor gate baseline (Q1 Stone 1, items 2 and 3). The one-time AUDIT of the raw-representation
# `::from_bits(` and decimal-parse `::from_decimal_str(` constructors in the non-exempt world-content
# modules (physics, sim, world; core and units are the quarantine, laws.rs and the loaders are exempt
# wholesale). Each row is CLASSIFIED, never a blanket freeze: the class column is one of
# engine-constant (a Principle-11 engine-mechanics constant: a half, an epsilon, a smallest-
# representable floor), bit-arithmetic (a Fixed reconstructed from a value computed in raw Q32.32 bits:
# a complement, a residue split, a bit-space mean), deserialization (a stored bit-pattern round-tripped
# back), test-fixture (a #[cfg(test)] constant or assertion), dev-fixture (a labelled non-canonical
# scenario or demo value, production-should-manifest-wire), or authored-should-derive (a value on the
# path of world content that must instead read from the floor: a SURFACED DEFECT, reported and to be
# fixed, never silently frozen). The gate fails on any count above a row's baseline (a new site to
# classify) or below it (update the row). Columns: pattern<TAB>path<TAB>count<TAB>class<TAB>reason.
# Audit result: 0 authored-should-derive rows. The canonical world-content path carries no inline
# authored value; every occurrence is engine-mechanics, bit-space arithmetic, deserialization, a test
# fixture, or a labelled dev-fixture. The dev-fixtures (the spawn_predator geometry and the test-
# scenario CreatureSelectionParams mutation spreads) are authored in the quarantined run harness and
# the test scenarios, not on a canonical law path; production wiring should read them from the manifest

column5: scaled-representation unit tests constructing a specific Q-format value from bits
LIFESPAN_HAZARD_THRESHOLD = 0.5 (1 << FRAC_BITS-1), a hazard midpoint
group-belief mean and variance accumulated in bit-space, plus a test tolerance

controller-weight mutation applied in bit-space (weight bits plus deviation bits)
decomposition drive computed in bit-space (temperature minus barrier), clamped

the conservative deep-time impact delta (excavated bowl balancing ejecta blanket, summing to zero) applied to the bombardment relief and the running surface in raw field bits, the bit-space redistribution the world crate's ballistic and redistribute chain produces
the derived molar gas constant R = N_A*k_B reconstructed from its rescaled Q32.32 bits (the same CODATA composite-compute projection as the derived Stefan-Boltzmann sigma)
evidence weight reconstructed from a value computed in bit-space
MIN_CAP ~1e-6, the smallest representable reserve capacity floor
apportionment residue split into exact bit-halves (the indivisible-residue handler)
damage and repair arithmetic in bit-space (complement, difference, abs), plus tiny test inputs
LOSS_THRESHOLD = 0.5, a labelled function-loss knee fixture
HALF (0.5) and HEADING_EPS (~1e-3), heading-quantisation constants
apportionment share bits and the level-of-detail wealth-proxy deserialization round-trip
damage bit-accumulation and the JITTER/GROWTH_LO/GROWTH_HI/HALF growth-band constants
a derived value reconstructed from bits, and a derived-Stefan-Boltzmann test assertion
promotion and stencil-stability constants, temperature-field deserialization, strike-damage bit-accumulation, and labelled AIR_K/WATER_K test fixtures
LADDER_WEIGHT_FLOOR = 1 bit, the smallest representable ladder weight
a decay complement computed in bit-space
a bit-scale tolerance in a transmission test
a bit-exact assertion in a typology test
a field cell average computed in bit-space (numerator over count)
the budget-conservation test reconstructs a Fixed from the per-cell field's bit-sum to compare against the ledger's ColumnSolid balance
saturating fixed-point add/subtract helpers rebuilding a Fixed from raw-bit saturating arithmetic (the runout integrator's determinism-safe ops), no authored value

the Born-exponent, structure-prototype, and ionic-lattice-energy-validation loaders round-trip cited decimal strings (Born exponents, Madelung constants, Born-Haber reference energies) from their TOML data files to fixed-point, the same discipline as the periodic-table and phase-registry loaders

the CODATA fundamentals N_A and k_B are round-tripped from the fundamentals registry to derive the molar gas constant R = N_A*k_B, the same derivation as environ.rs derived_gas_constant (which projects the composite bits); no authored value, the numbers are the fundamentals read from the floor
Fixed::from_bits(1).ln() = ln of the smallest positive Fixed, the log-space probability floor (ln_p_floor)
